title: Macramé storage basket
subtitle: A knotted round base and square-knot sides in 5 mm cotton cord
excerpt: Build a round base from spoke-warp cords radiating from a central ring, then add square-knot sides to make a freestanding storage basket about 20 cm across and 15 cm tall. The basket holds small items, yarn, or plant pots.
sourceNotes: Macramé basket construction synthesised from contemporary British macramé workshop practice. The spoke-base method is a standard basket-form technique adapted from traditional knotted-base work.

glossaryTerms.definition: The fundamental macramé knot made with two working cords around two filler cords. A half-square knot (left or right only) produces a spiral; alternating the direction for each knot produces the flat square knot.
A mounting knot used to attach cords to a ring, dowel, or another cord. The cord is folded in half, the loop is placed over the support, and both tails are pulled through the loop.
The passive centre cords in a square knot, over and around which the working cords are tied. The filler cords run straight and add the structural core to the knotted row.

content.text: This basket is built from a central ring from which the spoke cords radiate outward. The base is worked flat using

in concentric rows. Once the base is the desired diameter, the work is folded up and the sides are built with alternating square-knot rows. Use 5 mm single-strand cotton cord; it gives a firm structure that holds the basket shape once the tension is set.

items.name: Metal or wooden ring, 5 cm diameter (base centre)

content.text: Cut 16 cords, each 4 m long. Fold each in half and mount each pair onto the central ring using a
. This gives 32 working ends radiating from the ring like spokes. Lay the ring flat on the macramé board and pin it.
Work in groups of 4 cords (2 working, 2

), tying one square knot per group at about 3 cm from the ring. For the second row, split each group: take 2 cords from one group and 2 from the adjacent group to form new working groups of 4. Tie a square knot in each new group at about 3 cm from the first row. Continue alternating rows until the base reaches 20 cm across.

Fold the base upward at the perimeter to form the basket walls. Regroup the cords into sets of 4 and continue alternating square-knot rows, pulling the knots snugly so the sides rise vertically. Work 5 to 6 rows of alternating square knots for a 15 cm deep basket.

Tie an overhand knot in each cord at the top of the last square-knot row. Trim the cords to an even 5 cm fringe below the rim knots. Alternatively, fold the cord ends inside the basket and tuck them under the last row of knots to create a clean, fringeless top edge.

items.problem: The base is not flat and rises in the centre
items.cause: The first row of knots was too close to the ring, pulling the base into a dome
items.fix: Keep the first knot row at least 3 cm from the ring. If the base has already domed, wet the whole piece and press flat under a heavy book until dry.

items.problem: The sides lean outward instead of rising vertically
items.cause: The knots at the transition from base to sides were tied too loosely
items.fix: After the last base row, tighten all the transition knots firmly inward before beginning the side rows. The inward pull of tight knots at the fold line shapes the angle of the rise.
